Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»When is Akani Simbine in action at the Paris 2024 Olympics?

It’s almost go time for the quick men.

Akani Simbine will be one of the contenders to medal in the 100m sprint event at the Paris 2024 Olympics.

South African sprint sensation Akani Simbine ran a time of 9.93 seconds at Tokyo 2020 in the 100m final. That meant he missed a bronze medal by a heartbreaking four-hundredths of a second behind Canada’s Andre De Grasse. Italy’s Lamont Marcell Jacobs was the surprise gold medalist four years ago in a wide-open field that Simbine fancied his chances of winning in the Land of the Rising Sun. At Rio 2016; medalists in the 100m event were iconic Jamaican Usain Bolt with gold, USA’s Justin Gatlin claiming silver and De Grasse scooping the bronze. Rio and Tokyo contain painful memories for Akani Simbine, but Paris 2024 can change all of that.

30-year-old Akani Simbine has tied for fifth-fastest time this year, running 9.86 seconds two weeks before the Olympic Games. 21-year-old Benjamin Richardson and United States-based Shaun Maswanganyi will run with Simbine in the 4x100m event but the fourth member of what should be a formidable quartet is still unclear. Jamaican star Oblique Seville, who has a nod of approval from the iconic Usain Bolt, set the world’s fastest time of 2024, clocking 9.82 seconds in June.

Thepreliminary roundof theMen’s 100m event is on Saturday 03 August at 10:35 SA time. That’s followed byRound 1at11:45.Sunday 04 August sees the semi-finals at 20:00and then thegrand final,where Simbine will hope to line up, at21:50.Don’t miss it for the world.

South Africa has scooped three medals at the games so far. TheMen’s Rugby Sevensside beat Australia to claim bronze, whileAlan Hatherlywon bronze in the men’s Mountain Bike (MTB) cross-country race; South Africa’s first Olympic cycling medal since 1956.Tatjana Smith’s goldin the 100m breaststroke is the highlight.

author: James Freemantle
